hook: Your organization has a stronger 'why' than any Fortune 500 brand. Your marketing should act like it.
direction: Corporations invent purpose; ministries and mission-driven businesses have one. Yet their marketing is often the most timid in the room. Flip the frame: conviction is a competitive advantage.

facebook.text:
Corporations spend millions inventing a purpose. Mission statements workshopped in conference rooms, values printed on break-room posters.

Your organization never had that problem. The purpose came first. Everything else followed.

So it's strange that mission-driven marketing is often the most timid marketing in the room. Hedged language, borrowed templates, apology in the tone.

Conviction is a competitive advantage. You hold something worth saying plainly. Say it plainly.

instagram.text:
Corporations spend millions inventing a purpose. Your organization never had that problem — the purpose came first, and everything else followed.

So why is mission-driven marketing so often the most timid in the room? Hedged language, borrowed templates, apology in the tone.

Conviction is a competitive advantage. You hold something worth saying plainly. Say it plainly.

posts:
Corporations invent purpose. Ministries and mission-driven businesses are built on one.

So why is their marketing usually the most timid in the room?

Conviction is a competitive advantage. Act like it.

data.quote: Your organization has a stronger 'why' than any Fortune 500 brand. Your marketing should act like it.
